data.title: NSC praises Senate for maintaining steady funding for OSHA and NIOSH in legislation passed on Aug 1, 2025.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, the National Safety Council released a statement regarding the U.S. Senate Appropriations Committee's recent approval of legislation maintaining consistent funding levels for the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA). This decision ensures crucial financial support for OSHA's operations, benefiting workplace safety initiatives.

data.title: Arteris grants 109,000 RSUs to four new hires under 2022 Employee Inducement Incentive Plan.
data.description: Arteris, Inc., a renowned provider of system IP for advancing semiconductor production, disclosed on August 1, 2025, that it had awarded a total of 109,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) to four new recruits as inducement bonuses under the 2022 Employee Inducement Incentive Plan. The grants were sanctioned by the compensation committee of the firm’s board of directors and conformed to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

data.title: Trump fires Labor official, alleging rigged jobs numbers. Economists warned this move could erode data credibility.
data.description: Experts have warned that removing the leader of the Bureau of Labor Statistics may erode trust in official economic figures. The bureau plays a crucial role in analyzing and reporting labor market data, with over 2,400 employees working under its leadership. The possibility of this change has raised concerns about the credibility of government statistics among economists and the public.

data.title: Stock market drops after Trump tariffs, bad jobs report. Palantir, AMD lead $BIG earnings week.
data.description: On Friday, the stock market experienced a sell-off due to concerns over Trump's tariffs and a negative jobs report. As we approach another significant earnings week, companies like Palantir and AMD are in the spotlight. Following the market downturn, it is essential to evaluate your investment strategy. The Dow Jones Futures are especially crucial to monitor during this time. Stay informed about how companies such as Palantir and AMD are performing as they lead the way in these earnings reports. Keep an eye on market trends and make informed decisions to navigate the current financial landscape effectively.

data.title: Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara guarantees rate hikes but not more coverage after approving black-box wildfire model, says Consumer Watchdog.
data.description: On August 1, 2025 in Los Angeles, Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara is being criticized by Consumer Watchdog for reneging on his commitment to Californians. The watchdog group stated that Lara's approval of a new black-box wildfire model will result in rate increases without any assurance of expanded coverage. This decision comes despite Lara's promise to regulate insurance rates fairly. This recent development has raised concerns among consumers as it could lead to higher premiums without corresponding benefits.

data.title: STRC's offering broadens, accumulating BTC via equity and debt. Class action suits against the company on the rise.
data.description: The organization is persistently acquiring Bitcoin (BTC) via corporate equity and debt offerings. However, the number of class action lawsuits against the company known as Strategy is increasing.

data.title: PSEG advises rejecting TRC Capital's offer to buy up to 1.5 million shares of its stock.
data.description: In Newark, New Jersey on August 1, 2025, Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG) revealed an unsolicited mini-tender offer from TRC Capital Investment Corporation of Ontario, Canada. The offer involves the potential purchase of up to 1.5 million shares of PSEG common stock at a undisclosed price.

data.title: SEC Chair Aktins urges companies to bring crypto operations back to the US as global firms expand US operations, amid Trump's pro-crypto reforms.
data.description: Amid calls from officials to bring cryptocurrency operations back to the US, global companies are increasing their presence in the country. This move comes as the Trump administration introduces reforms supportive of cryptocurrencies. The push for reshoring crypto is gaining momentum, with companies expanding operations in the US in response to this trend. The administration's pro-crypto stance is attracting significant interest, and the number of companies relocating or establishing operations in the US is on the rise. Officials emphasize the importance of boosting domestic crypto capabilities, and the reforms introduced by the Trump administration are seen as conducive to this goal.
